commentary: GT1 vs the evolved champion (not the policy AI). Its genes say: ~16 str marching on each of 0211/0502, ~32 to deny 2311, 8 per 5-VP hex, one 26+ str field mass on my nearest credited hex, and real exits from GT12. So: no decoys, no eastern raid into a 32-str garrison. Fortress west in depth. 101-103 garrison 1115, 104-106 garrison 1108, Wilder+108 hold the 0822 shoulder, 112 onto 0502 with 113 in depth behind, 114 marches to physically occupy 0211 (odds-poisoning won't stop garrison-walkers), 109 cav and the exposed 110/111 come home to the fortress line before the CSA field mass picks its focus.

GT2 (resubmit - entry-turn reinforcements are not addressable, DSL v2 gap confirmed). Champion on-script: 2311 deny-garrison formed, 1920 garrisoned, main body wheeling NW. 110/111 keep running north ahead of Gracie; garrisons consolidate 1115/1108; 114 two turns from 0211. Reinforcements ride standing doctrine one turn - I take command of them GT3.

GT3. Improvised the missing hold-back verb: 147 stays ON entry hex 0728 to stall the XIV column off-board while Gracie's pack prowls the entry zone. Wilder+148+110/111 (20 str) converge on the isolated Brown/Bates stack at 0924 - defeat the western column in detail before it reaches 0211/0502. Fortress line holds: 101-103 on 1115 (their 34-str field mass is coming for it - one fight, then reassess), 104-106 on 1108, 108 keeps 0822, 112/113 hold the 0502 sector, 114 occupies 0211 this turn or next.

GT4. Brown+Bates destroyed for 8 VP, no losses - detail-kill doctrine validated against the champion. Entry block still on (147 holds 0728, whole queue safe off-board). Their field mass is at my 1108/1115 line; Kershaw (0917) and their artillery (1018) are isolated on my flank - Wilder+148+111 (16 str) take Kershaw first. 110 doubles the 0822 garrison against Gracie. 114 continues the race for 0211 against Trigg; 112/113 keep the 0502 sector. Front hexes stay 2-stacked with depth one hex back.

GT5. Champion's real effort is the NW: 26-str wing (Kershaw/Fulton/Law/Wilson/Wood + arty) driving on 0502, Trigg about to land on 0211. Checked the engine: unentered reinforcements are invisible to 17.32 - the entry block is VP-free insurance, stays on (147+149 sit on entry hexes). Wilder/111/148 stop chasing walkers and march to 0502 to meet the wing on the objective; 114 contests 0211 with 113 as the second echelon; 112 never leaves 0502. Eastern fortress unchanged - their center (~22) hasn't found odds against my 2-stacks.

GT6. 0211 secured - 114 on the hex, 113 closing as depth. Champion's 31-str west wing is 2-3 forest turns from 0502; Wilder's group ended up south of it on the west road, so everyone converges on the objective: 107/111/148 plus eastern depth 103/105 all march to 0502 to meet the wing ON the hex with 112. RELEASING the entry block - south zone is clear now that their army went north/west; 147 joins Wilder's echelon, 149 backs the 0822 garrison against Gracie+Deshler. The ~90-str queue floods in behind them and I take command of it next turn.

GT7. Kershaw killed on the march - 12-0, zero Union losses. Flood enters this turn (22 units, uncommandable until GT8; expect some doctrine wandering toward 1920 - Gracie at 0724 is the one wolf near the entry road, envelopment planned GT8). 0211: 113/114 hold the hex, 148 parks adjacent as counterattack depth against Trigg+Wood's 1:1. 0502 convergence continues: Wilder/111/103/105/147 all rally there with 112 before the 26-str wing arrives ~GT9. East stays quiet - 2-stacks hold.

GT8. Column drips 2/turn - no flood. NW is the whole game now: their ~30-str wing is 2 turns from 0502, their 17-str west-edge group (Trigg/Wood/Deshler/Kelly) threatens 0211 AND the 0111 exit hex (champion exits from GT12 - LOC plug duty noted for late cavalry). Convergence continues: 105 stacks 0502 with 112, 103/107/111/147 form the ring; 148 becomes 0211 depth. Fresh entrants shepherded away from Gracie: 150 (arty) west to 0422, 151 to the 0822 sector. East fortress unchanged.

GT9 night - free repositioning before the dawn assault on 0211. Wilder+111 swing in (park adjacent as counterattack force), 148 tightens: sector total ~25 vs their ~33 plus echelon. 103 and the XIV artillery continue to the 0502 ring - that assault is GT11-12, they are 8+ rows out. South consolidates on 0822 (147/149/151 join the 9-str stack) against Gracie and the CSA's own southern reinforcement drip. Exit insurance noted: if 0211 falls, late cavalry take LOC plug duty per 17.31.

GT10. Dawn assault on 0211 expected - defense set: 113/114 on the hex, Wilder+111 ON exit hex 0111 (LOC plug doubles as flank), 148 at 0210, 103 blocking the 0402 approach, 0502 keeps its 11-str stack plus arty inbound. South consolidates 24 str on the 0822 sector. COUNTERSTROKE opens: their entire army is west, 1920 is held by 6 str - XXI entrants route northeast (153 to the 1123 waypoint, partners follow as they enter) to take it late, when nothing of theirs can march back in time.

GT11. The champion is odds-locked: 50 str hovering at rows 13-15 because my 0111/0210/0211 stacks deny the adjacency it needs for 3:1. Nobody moves in the NW - the hover costs them the game clock. 152 thickens the 0502 ring at 0402. South depth units switch to plain hold (stop bouncing off the full 0822 stack). Counterstroke: 109 cav drops to 1023 to pair with 153 (lone 5-str won't brush Anderson6/Deas solo); XXI followers join as they enter, then 1920 together.

GT12. Champion exit window opened but its genome only exits units ALREADY on exit hexes - Wilder squats on 0111 (plug + LOC void insurance). 1920 raid downgraded to a pinning threat: their east response force is ~25, my raid would be 9-14; 153/155/109 assemble around 1123-1223 to freeze Forrest/Scott/Jackson in place without closing. NW fortress holds the 12-0 lock; 152 deepens 0402, arty closes on the 0502 ring; 147 finishes the 0822 consolidation.

GT13. Key insight banked for doctrine: the champion genome is enemy-blind - garrison demand doesn't scale with my posture, so pinning feints are worthless against it; only hex credit and forced combat register. Therefore: raid trio (109/153/155) abandons the east threat and reinforces the 1108/1115 line against Benning/Ector's 1408 lunge risk. 0502 braces for the GT14 assault: 103 steps onto approach hex 0402 to cut their adjacency count, 11-str stack holds the hex, arty and 152 still closing. Everything else: fortress discipline, protect 12-0.

GT14. The 0502 attack lands this turn at ~2:1 vs my 11-str stack with 103 denying the 0402 approach - no reinforcement can physically arrive, it's held by geometry and dice now; if it falls, GT15 counterattack with 103 + survivors. 0822 sector at parity vs ~27. Wilder stays welded to 0111 (their exit account stays void). Entrants: 156 to the 0822 shield, 157 to 1115. Trio finishes closing the east line. One turn to hold 12-0.

GT15 final. Their 0502 assault never found odds - one last approach-hex denial: 103 steps 0402->0503 against the N/E weight. 0822 ring tightens (149/151 park on the open flanks, 156 closes). East trio and 157 finish garrison depth at 1108/1115 - no CSA unit is within reach of them. Wilder immovable on 0111: exit account void, LOC plugged. Scoring check: 1920/2311 are Union-scoring only, so CSA's deny-garrisons earn zero; if the line holds one more Confederate turn this ends ~27-0.
